image.alt: Valley Orthodontics modern, welcoming treatment area with bright chairs and smiling team

paragraphs.text: Welcome to Valley Orthodontics. Our mission is to create healthy, beautiful smiles in a caring, comfortable environment where each patient receives individualized, up-to-date orthodontic care.

We treat children and adults using advanced digital tools like a 3-D Itero scanner, digital 3-D X-rays, and modern clear aligner workflows so your treatment is accurate, efficient, and as comfortable as possible.

items.title: Technology
items.text: State-of-the-art digital tools, including 2-D/3-D X-rays and the Itero 3D scanner, for precise diagnoses and more comfortable, efficient treatment.

items.title: Experience
items.text: Orthodontic specialists with years of focused training who design personalized treatment plans for predictable, long-lasting results.

items.title: Caring & Community
items.text: A friendly, patient-centered team that treats each person with respect and supports Marin community outreach and sponsorships.

paragraphs.text: Dr. Charlene Rocha, DDS, MSD, is a specialist in orthodontics for children and adults. A Bay Area native, she earned her Doctor of Dental Surgery degree at the University of California, San Francisco and completed her master’s and specialty training in orthodontics at University of the Pacific. Dr. Rocha combines advanced clinical skills with a warm, patient-first approach, offering both braces and clear aligner options and tailoring care to each patient’s needs. She volunteers in community outreach and participates in local health events, and she is a member of the American Dental Association, California Dental Association, and the Marin County Dental Society.

We offer a full range of orthodontic services for children and adults, including removable clear aligners, modern bracket systems, growth-guiding appliances, and combined orthodontic-surgical care when indicated.

og_meta.title: Home - Valley Orthodontics
og_meta.description: Valley Orthodontics in San Rafael provides Invisalign, braces, expanders, and advanced digital orthodontics with compassionate, personalized care.

image.alt: Valley Orthodontics team smiling in a bright, modern treatment area

paragraphs.text: At Valley Orthodontics, our mission is to provide exceptional orthodontic care paired with a personal, meaningful experience for every patient we have the privilege to serve. We are dedicated to creating healthy, beautiful smiles in an environment that is caring and welcoming.

Our focus is on delivering meticulous service and the highest standard of care to every member of our orthodontic family. It is an honor to serve patients and their families with compassion, respect, and a heartfelt commitment to overall well‑being.

Valley Orthodontics evaluates the whole face and favors non‑extraction treatment when possible to achieve balanced faces, full smiles, and healthy bites. We use high‑quality appliances and current technology to deliver personalized care tailored to each patient.

image.alt: Dr. Charlene Rocha smiling in the Valley Orthodontics office

paragraphs.text: Dr. Charlene Rocha, DDS, MSD, is a specialist in orthodontics who treats children and adults using braces and clear aligners. She brings years of clinical experience and a patient‑centered approach to individualized treatment plans.

Dr. Rocha completed her Doctor of Dental Surgery and orthodontic specialty training, earning a Master of Science in orthodontics. Her advanced training included hands‑on experience with both traditional braces and modern clear aligner systems.

image.alt: Dr. Rocha in a treatment room

paragraphs.text: Dr. Rocha is a certified Invisalign<sup>®</sup> provider and an early adopter of digital tools like the iTero 3‑D scanner, which helps create more accurate, comfortable treatment plans for clear aligners and braces.

image.alt: Dr. Rocha discussing treatment options with a patient

paragraphs.text: Outside the office, Dr. Rocha enjoys exploring local parks with her family, running half marathons, listening to audiobooks and podcasts, and trying new restaurants. She values long‑term patient relationships and community connection.

image.alt: Valley Orthodontics staff at a community event

paragraphs.text: Valley Orthodontics supports local youth sports and community programs, including Dixie/Terra Linda Boys Baseball, San Rafael Girls Softball League, and the Marinwood Waterdevils. We also run seasonal events like a Halloween Candy Buy‑Back to give back to families.

image.alt: Staff volunteering at a local event

paragraphs.text: Dr. Rocha and the team participate in outreach trips, such as volunteering in Honduras to provide free dental care, and regularly take part in regional health fairs to improve access to dental services for underserved communities.

items.name: Anna Bacon
items.bio: Anna manages the front office, scheduling, treatment planning, financial consultations, and insurance requests. She holds a BA in Communications from UC Santa Barbara and enjoys hiking and road trips.

items.name: Carrie
items.bio: Carrie supports patient care coordination, insurance questions, and new patient onboarding. She is a reliable point of contact for scheduling and patient communication.

We volunteer at local health fairs, support youth sports, and travel on outreach trips to provide free dental care to underserved communities. Our programs include local sponsorships and seasonal events such as a Halloween Candy Buy‑Back.

Get Started.title: What to expect at your first visit

paragraphs.text: Your first visit begins with a review of your completed new patient forms and a short conversation about your dental and medical history. We will take photographs and perform a complete oral exam to assess whether orthodontic treatment is appropriate for you or your child.

If treatment is recommended, we will discuss available options, create a customized treatment plan with an estimated length of care, review financial details including insurance benefits and payment plans, and answer any questions you have.

image.alt: Orthodontic consultation with patient and clinician reviewing records

Get Started.title: Payment options, insurance providers accepted, financing plans, and complimentary consultation offers

paragraphs.text: We accept most major insurance plans and will file claims electronically when possible to make your visits easier.

Flexible payment plans are available, and we offer a complimentary consultation so you can review treatment options and financing. Book your free consultation at https://www.valleyorthodontics.net/complimentary-consultation/

Please complete the Medical and Dental History form before your appointment so we can provide safe, personalized care. You can fill out our digital New Patient Information form online at https://p.adit.com/1xX3r.

If you prefer, we can mail forms to you or you may download and bring completed paperwork to your visit. Bringing finished forms helps your appointment run smoothly and keeps your first visit efficient.

image.alt: Patient consulting with orthodontic staff and completing new patient paperwork

image.alt: Smiling patients of all ages at Lakeshore Orthodontics

paragraphs.text: The most common type of braces, modern metal brackets are smaller and more comfortable than before. They provide reliable, efficient tooth movement for children, teens, and adults who want a predictable path to a straight smile.

image.alt: Close-up of traditional metal braces on teeth

paragraphs.text: Ceramic or tooth-colored braces blend with your smile for a less noticeable look while still delivering effective tooth movement. They are a popular choice for patients seeking a more discreet fixed appliance.
Clear aligners use a series of removable, custom trays to straighten teeth gradually. They let you eat and clean normally and are an excellent option for patients who want a low‑profile treatment.

image.alt: Two stylish smiling teens taking a selfie

paragraphs.text: Invisalign<sup>®</sup> uses 3‑D imaging to create a sequence of custom clear aligners. Each tray is worn for about two weeks, allowing discreet, controlled movement with minimal impact on daily life.

image.alt: Close-up of clear aligners being placed on teeth

paragraphs.text: Iconix Champagne Gold Braces combine the strength of metal brackets with a subtle, champagne‑gold finish for a more cosmetic appearance while maintaining dependable tooth movement.
The Carriere Self‑Ligating Bracket System is designed for comfort and efficiency. By reducing friction and the need for elastic ties, it can shorten treatment time for many patients.

image.alt: Orthodontic office interior showing treatment equipment

paragraphs.text: A palatal expander gently widens the upper jaw by applying pressure to the molars at adjustment. It creates space for alignment and can help correct crossbites and crowded bites when used at the right stage of development.

Surgical orthodontics, or orthognathic surgery, is used to correct severe jaw and bite discrepancies. We coordinate closely with oral and maxillofacial surgeons to plan combined surgical and orthodontic care when it will improve function and facial balance.

image.alt: Orthodontic patient receiving orthognathic surgical consultation

image.alt: Patient smiling with orthodontic braces in our clinic

paragraphs.text: Find quick answers to common questions about braces care, first-day expectations, troubleshooting, instructional videos, forms, scheduling, and billing at Valley Orthodontics.

If a part of your braces or your retainer breaks, save the piece and call to schedule a repair. Some appliances can be repaired quickly; others need a replacement. If a wire or bracket is irritating, place orthodontic wax over the area and call us so we can advise whether you need a short appointment.

Mild soreness is common. Check for anything rubbing a cheek or lip and cover it with wax. Rinse with warm salt water to soothe tender spots, and take a photo of the area to send to the office if you’re unsure. Call us if pain increases, swelling develops, or you need further guidance.

accordion.header: What to expect on the first day of braces

paragraphs.text: The bonding appointment usually lasts 1.5 to 2.5 hours, and most patients can return to school or work the same day. The bonding adhesives reach maximum strength in about 24 hours, so stick to soft foods for the first few days. Mild soreness can appear a few hours after placement and often lasts up to three days; ibuprofen (Advil or Motrin) taken as directed can help.

We have short how-to videos you can watch anytime: Broken Brackets (https://www.valleyorthodontics.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Broken-Brackets.mp4), How to Use Wax (https://www.valleyorthodontics.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Wax-patient-comfort.mp4), Placing Separators (https://www.valleyorthodontics.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Elastic-Tooth-Separator.mp4), What to Expect - Braces (https://www.valleyorthodontics.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/bracket-bonding.mp4), and What to Expect - First Orthodontic (https://www.valleyorthodontics.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/what-to-expect.mp4). These show simple, clinic-tested steps you can follow at home.

Complete your New Patient Information before your first visit using our secure digital form: https://p.adit.com/1xX3r. Completing this ahead of time speeds check-in and lets us review medical and dental history before you arrive.

To book a complimentary consultation or ask billing questions, call (415) 479-2400 or visit our booking page: https://www.valleyorthodontics.net/complimentary-consultation/. We’ll review insurance coverage and available payment options during your visit—call ahead if you’d like an estimate before scheduling.
